evidence: Build-time config-schema breaking-diff gate verified on merged main. Gates: npm run build / check / test all exit 0 (check-config-schema.test.ts 13/13 via the root test:migration glob). Runtime demos with the real gate script pre-merge: clean staged + --base origin/main modes exit 0; a staged removal of properties.lenses exited 1 naming the JSON path and the expand-contract message; the same removal paired with a version bump to 2.0.0 + a packaged config migration declaration exited 0; files restored byte-clean. Wired as the 5th pre-commit line and a pull_request CI step; the introducing commit itself passed through the full hook including the new gate. Baseline determination recorded: prior git revision (staged HEAD vs working tree; --base ref vs HEAD for CI), mirroring check-changelog's two-mode model.

criteria_results.criterion: A non-additive config.schema.json diff absent a paired migration fails the build-time gate
criteria_results.evidence: Live demo: staged removal of properties.lenses -> exit 1 naming $.properties.lenses + the unpaired reason; tests cover removed root/nested keys, renames-as-removals, new required on pre-existing objects, bump-without-decl, decl-without-bump

criteria_results.criterion: An additive diff (a new optional field) passes the gate
criteria_results.evidence: Tests: new optional property, description/enum additions, version-only change, and a newly-added optional object with initial requireds all produce zero findings; clean-tree live runs exit 0

criteria_results.criterion: New config fields land optional; a removal or rename is gated to a contract release
criteria_results.evidence: The discipline is mechanical: breaking findings pass only with a schema version advance plus a packaged config migration declaration reaching the new version (live-demonstrated); documented in the README schema-versioning paragraph, the CLAUDE.md gate enumeration, and the CHANGELOG entry
